test_gap_typed_arrays.reason: Uint8ClampedArray index-set does NOT clamp values. Repro: `const c = new Uint8ClampedArray(3); c[0] = 300; c[1] = -10; c[2] = 128; console.log(c[0], c[1], c[2])` prints `300 -10 128` (Perry) vs `255 0 128` (Node). v0.5.380 / b2b0a3e6 added Uint8ClampedArray-specific intercepts at the start of `Expr::IndexGet` (line ~1687) and `Expr::IndexSet` (line ~2353) in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs` that route to `js_typed_array_get`/`js_typed_array_set` (which DO apply ToUint8Clamp via `KIND_UINT8_CLAMPED` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/typedarray.rs::store_at` line ~244). But the test still fails on clean main HEAD — the IndexSet path the test takes bypasses the new intercept. Likely culprits: (a) `receiver_class_name(ctx, object)` returns None for the test's `LocalGet`-typed receiver, (b) `lower_index_set_fast` or another inline-store fast path fires before the new intercept arm, (c) the receiver's `Type::Named("Uint8ClampedArray")` info is lost between binding and use. Investigation: dump LLVM IR via `PERRY_NO_CACHE=1 PERRY_SAVE_LL=/tmp/dump.ll target/release/perry test-files/test_gap_typed_arrays.ts -o /tmp/x` and check whether the call site is `@js_typed_array_set` (correct) or `@js_array_set_f64`/inline `store double` (bug). Tracked separately as a follow-up to b2b0a3e6.

test_issue_154_using_dispose.reason: SIGBUS (exit code 138) silently before producing any output. Repro: `target/release/perry test-files/test_issue_154_using_dispose.ts -o /tmp/t154 && /tmp/t154; echo $?` prints nothing then `138`. Pre-existing crash, acknowledged in CLAUDE.md v0.5.377 entry: "The pre-existing test_issue_154_using_dispose SIGBUS is unrelated to this change (reproduces on clean main HEAD pre-fix)." The `using` / `await using` desugar landed in v0.5.317 via `crates/perry-hir/src/lower_decl.rs::lower_stmts_using_aware` (rewires block-stmt lowering to wrap each binding in nested `Stmt::Try { catch: None, finally: ... }`). Likely failure mode: a runtime dispose call dereferences a bad pointer (NaN-boxed undefined misread as `*StringHeader` or `*ClosureHeader`), or the synthesized `try/finally` lowering produces a malformed exception-frame that crashes during stack unwinding. Investigation: lldb the binary at `/tmp/t154`, look for the crashing instruction; likely lives in `crates/perry-runtime/src/exception.rs` or the dispose-method dispatch in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs`. Tracked separately.
